content: I've been training for my first triathlon next month and I'm really struggling with the swim portion. Any tips for improving my freestyle technique?
That's exciting about your first triathlon! For freestyle technique, focus on your body rotation and breathing rhythm. Have you tried bilateral breathing yet?

I've been practicing bilateral breathing but I keep getting water up my nose. I work at a sustainable design firm in Pittsburgh and our office has a gym with a pool, so I try to swim during lunch breaks when I'm not traveling to construction sites.
Water up the nose is a common issue! Try exhaling gently through your nose while your face is in the water. How often are you swimming each week?

I try to swim 3 times a week, but sometimes construction meetings run long. On weekends I'm usually restoring my 1967 Mustang with my roommate Marcus. He's teaching me about carburetors while I teach him salsa dancing.
That's quite the skill exchange! For your swimming, consistency is key. Have you considered joining a masters swim group? They often have coached workouts that could help with your technique.

I've thought about it but my schedule is so unpredictable with client meetings. I'm also taking painting classes on Thursday evenings - I'm working on a series of urban landscapes of Pittsburgh's bridges.
That sounds like a full plate! Maybe try some dryland exercises to supplement your swimming? Core work and shoulder stability exercises can really help with your swim performance.

Good idea. I've been doing yoga with my other roommate Priya, who's a nurse at UPMC. She's been helping me with recovery nutrition too. I'm trying to figure out what to eat before morning swim sessions.
Priya sounds like a great resource! For morning swims, try something easily digestible like a banana with almond butter or a small smoothie about 30 minutes before. What distance is your triathlon?
It's an Olympic distance - 1.5K swim, 40K bike, 10K run. I'm most nervous about the open water swim since I've only trained in the pool. The race is in Lake Arthur on June 15th.
That's a great distance for your first triathlon! For open water, practice sighting every 6-8 strokes and try to stay relaxed. Have you done any open water practice yet?
Not yet, but I'm planning to go to Moraine State Park next weekend for my first open water session. I'm a bit nervous about the mass start though.
